data.title: In the past 7 days, only 11 public blockchains have generated more than $100,000 in revenue.
data.description: According to Nansen data, only 11 public blockchains generated over $100,000 in fee revenue over the past 7 days, with only 6 of them exceeding $1 million. Tron topped the list with $6.16 million in fee revenue, followed by Ethereum ($3.87 million), Solana ($2.94 million), BNB Chain ($2.65 million), Bitcoin ($1.78 million), and Base Network ($1.13 million). Additionally, HyperEVM generated $406,000 in fee revenue over the past 7 days, and the newly launched Monad blockchain project generated $111,000.

data.title: Ansem: 95% of crypto projects are "junk," future value will only belong to BTC, stablecoins, and a few other sectors.
data.description: Crypto trader Ansem posted on social media that the main value accumulation phase of the crypto industry is "basically over," and the vast majority of tokens ("95% junk") will struggle to gain sustained value in the future. He stated that what will truly capture value in the future will be stablecoins and the blockchain infrastructure built on the proprietary chains of traditional fintech companies like Stripe, Coinbase, and Robinhood, rather than most token projects currently on the market. Ansem believes that only three areas still offer investment opportunities now and in the future: Bitcoin, privacy-focused sectors, and the Hype/DEX ecosystem. He added that a new wave of opportunities may emerge after 2027, stemming from the combination of AI/robotics and blockchain.

data.title: South African Reserve Bank Evaluates CBDC Necessity Amid Payment System Modernization
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the South African Reserve Bank (SARB) has expressed that there is no immediate requirement for a central bank digital currency (CBDC) in the near term, emphasizing the need to modernize the country's payment system instead. In a paper released on Thursday, the central bank stated that while deploying a retail CBDC is technically feasible, there is no "strong immediate need" for it. The SARB highlighted existing initiatives aimed at modernizing the payments system and expanding non-bank participation in the national payment system as current priorities.

The SARB clarified that although it does not currently advocate for the implementation of a retail CBDC, it remains vigilant in monitoring developments and is prepared to act if necessary. The central bank plans to shift its focus towards exploring wholesale CBDC applications and enhancing cross-border payment efficiency, while continuing to observe retail CBDC advancements. The research conducted by the SARB examined whether a retail CBDC could address existing gaps in South Africa's payment system, noting that challenges persist with approximately 16% of adults remaining unbanked.

For a CBDC to be successful, it would need to offer benefits that match or exceed those of cash, including offline functionality, universal acceptance, low costs, ease of use, and privacy features. The SARB also issued a warning regarding crypto and stablecoins, identifying them as potential financial risks. In a report released earlier this week, the central bank flagged "crypto assets and stablecoins" as a new risk associated with technology-enabled financial innovation. Additionally, the bank cautioned that cryptocurrencies could be used to circumvent Exchange Control Regulations, which manage the inflows and outflows of funds to South Africa.

Globally, the race to develop CBDCs continues, with only three countries—Nigeria, Jamaica, and The Bahamas—having officially launched a CBDC, according to the Atlantic Council CBDC Tracker. Currently, 49 countries are in the pilot testing phase, 20 countries are actively developing one, and 36 countries are researching a CBDC. Meanwhile, the United States has shelved its CBDC plans under the Trump administration, as the global pursuit of CBDCs persists.

data.title: Monochrome Bitcoin ETF Reaches Significant Holdings
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the Australian Monochrome Spot Bitcoin ETF (IBTC) has disclosed that its holdings have reached 1,158 bitcoins. The market value of these holdings has surpassed 161 million Australian dollars.

data.title: Matrixport Group and its employees jointly donated HK$3 million to support fire relief and reconstruction efforts in Hong Kong.
data.description: To fully support emergency relief, disaster relief, and reconstruction efforts following the Tai Po fire in Hong Kong, Matrixport Group, through its Hong Kong branch, donated HK$3 million to relevant relief organizations. This donation, jointly made by the Group and its employees, will be specifically used for disaster relief and reconstruction. Following the disaster, the Group immediately communicated and coordinated with relevant Hong Kong organizations to swiftly implement the donation. Matrixport expresses its deepest condolences to the citizens who tragically lost their lives and the firefighters who perished in the accident, pays its highest respects to the frontline firefighters and paramedics, and extends its sincere sympathies to the families of the victims. Matrixport will continue to closely monitor the disaster's progress and collaborate with local charitable partners to provide ongoing support to the affected Hong Kong communities.

data.title: Crypto Wins Big: Thailand Moves To A 0% Tax On Local Exchange Gains
data.description:
Thailand has officially adopted a new tax-rule giving a 0% personal income tax rate on capital gains from cryptocurrency trades — but only under certain conditions. Related Reading: Bitcoin Faces More Downside After Recent Crash, Data Shows According to regulation Ministerial Regulation No. 399 (MR 399), profits earned from selling or transferring cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin via exchanges, brokers, or dealers licensed by the Securities and Exchange Commission of Thailand (SEC) will be tax-free from January 1, 2025 until December 31, 2029. What The 0% Tax Means Under the new scheme, individual investors who trade crypto through SEC-licensed platforms don’t pay personal income tax on any gains
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/altcoin/crypto-wins-big-thailand-moves-to-a-0-tax-on-local-exchange-gains/

data.title: Bitcoin Network Faces High Security Amid Low Profitability
data.description:
According to PANews, the Bitcoin network is currently experiencing a paradoxical phase characterized by high security and low profitability. The network's hash rate remains stable above 1 zettahash, a historical peak, while miner revenue per unit of computation has plummeted, leading to a structural reshuffle within the industry.

On November 27, Bitcoin mining difficulty decreased by 2% at block height 925344, reaching 149.30 trillion, marking the second adjustment of the month. Despite this, the block interval remains close to the 10-minute target. The 'hash price,' a key indicator of miner revenue, has dropped by 50% in recent weeks, hitting a record low of $34.20 per petabyte per second.

This contrast between high hash rates and low earnings stems from a polarization among miners. Smaller miners unable to secure cheap electricity are exiting the market, while larger operators with long-term power agreements and off-grid power stations continue to expand.

Even stablecoin giant Tether has paused its mining project in Uruguay due to uncertainties in energy costs and tariffs, reflecting the survival pressures on small and medium-sized miners. Although the hash rate appears unchanged, it is a result of industry consolidation, with the number of entities supporting network security significantly decreasing.

The trend towards centralization poses risks, as single factors like extreme weather or power grid limitations could trigger chain reactions. The capital market has already reacted, with the market value of listed mining companies evaporating by nearly $30 billion in November, dropping from a peak of $87 billion to $55 billion before slightly rebounding to $65 billion.

Investors' perceptions of mining companies are also shifting, viewing them less as 'Bitcoin substitutes' and more as data center businesses with added crypto attributes.

Western miners are exploring new revenue streams by signing long-term power contracts, relocating to flexible grid areas, or taking on artificial intelligence and high-performance computing (HPC) orders.

To gauge the industry's direction, three key indicators should be monitored: a significant decrease in mining difficulty would confirm the exit of high-cost miners, while a rebound would indicate the restart of idle capacity; transaction fees rising due to memory pool congestion could temporarily improve earnings; and policy changes, such as export controls or grid rule adjustments, could instantly alter cost structures.

The current paradox in the Bitcoin network is stark, with protocol-level security at an all-time high due to high hash rates, yet the underlying mining industry faces capital liquidation and consolidation pressures. If financial constraints and high energy costs persist, the industry may see more mergers and relocations. Conversely, if Bitcoin prices and fees rebound, some idle capacity may restart, but ownership and operational models have fundamentally changed.
